title: Fix CC3 skills for Pi-agent compatibility
description:
Pi-agent has stricter skill validation than Claude Code. The CC3 skills have frontmatter issues that cause warnings on Pi-agent startup.

## Issues to Fix

### Critical (breaks loading)
1. **Name collisions** (4 skills) - archive/ folder has duplicates:
   - async-repl-protocol
   - router-first-architecture  
   - search-hierarchy
   - search-tools
   
2. **Names with underscores** (8 skills) - must be lowercase a-z, 0-9, hyphens only:
   - continuity_ledger → continuity-ledger
   - create_handoff → create-handoff
   - describe_pr → describe-pr
   - implement_plan → implement-plan
   - implement_plan_micro → implement-plan-micro
   - implement_task → implement-task
   - resume_handoff → resume-handoff
   - system_overview → system-overview

3. **Missing descriptions** (8 skills):
   - loogle-search, recall, remember, system_overview
   - tldr-deep, tldr-overview, tldr-router, tour

4. **Name mismatches** (4 skills) - frontmatter name ≠ directory name:
   - math-unified has name: math
   - agent-browser has name: Agent Browser
   - self-improving-agent has name: self-improvement
   - superdesign has name: frontend-design

### Warnings (noisy but non-blocking)
- ~60 skills have unknown frontmatter fields: user-invocable, triggers, priority, keywords, model, version, type, homepage, read_when

## Proposed Fix Order
1. Remove archive/ folder (fixes collisions immediately)
2. Rename underscore directories to hyphens
3. Add missing descriptions
4. Fix name mismatches in frontmatter
5. (Optional) Remove unknown frontmatter fields or update Pi-agent to ignore them
close_reason: All critical Pi-compat issues resolved: archive/ gone (V4), underscores renamed (V4), descriptions present, names match dirs, duplicate agent-browser removed, last30days variant name fixed. ~60 unknown frontmatter fields are non-blocking warnings — not worth churn to remove.

title: Fix pi skill validation errors
description:
Fixed skill configuration issues causing pi validation failures:

## Issues Fixed

### 1. compound-docs (2 locations)
- **Error**: unknown frontmatter field 'preconditions'
- **Fix**: Removed invalid `preconditions:` field from YAML frontmatter
- **Files**: 
  - ~/.pi/agent/skills/compound-docs/SKILL.md
  - ~/.pi/agent/skills/compound/compound-docs/SKILL.md (symlink to same source)

### 2. create-agent-skills (2 locations)
- **Error**: name 'creating-agent-skills' does not match parent directory 'create-agent-skills'
- **Fix**: Changed `name: creating-agent-skills` to `name: create-agent-skills`
- **Files**:
  - ~/.pi/agent/skills/create-agent-skills/SKILL.md
  - ~/.pi/agent/skills/compound/create-agent-skills/SKILL.md (symlink to same source)

### 3. last30days (2 locations)
- **Error**: Multiple unknown frontmatter fields (argument-hint, context, agent)
- **Fix**: 
  - Removed invalid fields: `argument-hint`, `context`, `agent`, `disable-model-invocation`
  - Moved argument hint into description field
  - Fixed `allowed-tools` format from comma-separated to YAML list
- **Files**:
  - ~/.pi/agent/skills/last30days/SKILL.md
  - ~/.pi/agent/skills/tools/last30days/SKILL.md (symlink to same source)

## Valid Pi Skill Frontmatter Fields
- `name` (required) - lowercase, hyphens, max 64 chars
- `description` (required) - max 1024 chars
- `allowed-tools` (optional) - YAML list format
- `model` (optional) - specific model to use

## Source Files
All skills symlink to actual sources in Dropbox:
- compound-docs → compound-engineering-plugin
- create-agent-skills → compound-engineering-plugin
- last30days → last30days-skill
close_reason: All skill validation errors resolved. Fixes applied to source files via symlinks.

title: Track Claude hooks in agent-config repo
description: settings.json references 28 hook files in ~/.claude/hooks/ but hooks are not tracked in agent-config. A fresh machine cannot reach working state from the repo alone — hooks must be manually rsync'd. This breaks the core promise of agent-config: one clone, one setup, everything works. Need to bring hooks source into the repo, make bootstrap own the build lifecycle, and add integration tests that verify config dependencies resolve.
close_reason: Shape A implemented and verified — hooks source tracked in configs/claude/hooks/, bootstrap.sh handles sync+install+build, verify-hooks.sh integration test passes on both laptop (32/32) and Mini (32/32, 30/30 bootstrap check)

title: Agent config portability: eliminate external symlinks, functional taxonomy
description: Shape D from spec 003. V1: clean broken symlinks, V2: submodule skill-repos, V3: vendor remaining externals, V4: taxonomy restructure. See specs/003-agent-config-portability/
notes: V4 complete (ed5b03e). Taxonomy restructure: 222 skills moved from origin-based (cc3/personal/ralph-o/compound/swift) to functional categories (tools/review/workflows/meta/domain). 303 skill files preserved, 256 discovery symlinks, 0 broken. All 4 slices done.
close_reason: All 4 slices complete. V1: broken symlinks cleaned. V2: submodules for skill-repos. V3: vendor remaining externals. V4: functional taxonomy restructure. Agent-config is fully self-contained and portable.

title: Integrate Xcode26-Agent-Skills into unified config
close_reason: Already integrated. xcode-26 lives at skills/domain/swift/xcode-26/ with SKILL.md + 20 reference files + top-level discovery symlink. Vendored in V3 (fb099e1), moved to taxonomy in V4 (ed5b03e).

title: Create /issue command: unified shape → spec → review → implement pipeline
description: After thousands of sessions, the specs/ workflow (numbered spec/plan/tasks) works great when agents follow it, but they often drift — making just a plan or PRD instead. Build a single /issue command that orchestrates: bead creation → shaping (full, interactive) → spec creation (specs/<NNN>-<slug>/) → codex-review → workflows-work. Key challenge: agents tend to do half-hearted shaping instead of following the actual /shaping skill protocol.
close_reason: Implemented /shape and /issue commands. /shape enforces full shaping protocol via forced SKILL.md read with anti-pattern callouts. /issue creates bead + numbered specs/<NNN>-<slug>/ with spec/plan/tasks. Reviewed by QuickPhoenix, built by IronMoon.

title: Fix finalize/handoff/checkpoint context + GH close flow
description:
Spec:
- Add session-summary extraction to prevent finalize amnesia (infer bead + outcome from transcript).
- Infer primary bead automatically; only ask if ambiguous.
- Propose outcome with reasoning; allow user override.
- Finalize must close linked GH issue (use bead external-ref or set then close).
- Make artifact editing trustworthy: validate mode + primary_bead + filename, only edit returned artifact.
- Avoid prompt-time $VAR expansion (use Python wrapper, no $ARTIFACT_PATH).
- Commit/push only if explicitly requested.
close_reason: All 7 spec items already implemented: session-summary extraction, auto bead inference, outcome proposal with override, GH issue close flow, artifact validation (mode+bead+filename), Python wrapper (no $VAR expansion), explicit commit/push. Commands are live and working.

title: Allow .learnings append and log oracle path error
Automate external config bootstrap for cross-machine parity
description: Create a deterministic bootstrap/check flow for parity surfaces outside ~/.agent-config (codex/opencode config files and optional plugin-generated dirs), so new machines can intentionally match approved baseline without copying accidental laptop drift.
close_reason: Bootstrap system complete: configs/ baselines + scripts/bootstrap.sh check/apply/status. 28/28 checks pass. Pi extension packages excluded (per-machine install).

title: codex-review rev 2 + post-commit hook + slash command resolution
description: Rewrote /codex-review command (rev 2) to fix 65% self-approval rate. Added post-commit hook for Gemini TOML regen + symlink validation. Set up dual-push to mini-ts with post-receive hook. Added §9 slash command resolution to AGENTS.md.
close_reason: Completed — codex-review rev 2, post-commit hooks, slash command resolution all shipped

title: OpenClaw Mac mini agent-config parity rollout
description: Plan and execute first-time install of ~/.agent-config on OpenClaw Mac mini with validated parity against laptop command/instruction/skill surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: Bead-linked spec/plan/tasks created; parity checks defined; install and verification runbook shaped with rollback and risk controls.
notes:
Phase 1 laptop baseline captured at specs/001-openclaw-mac-mini-parity-rollout/artifacts/phase1-laptop-baseline-2026-02-28 (snapshot/report/summary). Observed managed drifts: claude_instructions=drift_target, pi_commands=missing.
Phase 2/3 executed on mini-ts. install.sh + install-all.sh logs captured in specs/001-openclaw-mac-mini-parity-rollout/artifacts/phase2-mini-install-2026-02-28. Managed surfaces all ok; blocker remains bunx/bun missing (compound plugin skipped), plus bd/rg missing for strict tool parity.
User clarified laptop is not canonical. Treat repo-defined installer state as target and clean laptop drift. Remaining blockers for strict tool parity on mini: bun/bunx, bd, rg missing.
Phase 4 final parity verification captured at specs/001-openclaw-mac-mini-parity-rollout/artifacts/phase4-final-parity-2026-02-28. Managed surfaces aligned across laptop + mini at SHA 69d12dc. Non-blocking external drift captured and follow-up bead opened: .agent-config-gyi.
close_reason: Phase 1-4 parity rollout complete; residual external drift moved to .agent-config-gyi

title: Add /sweep, /audit-agents, and /implement slash commands
close_reason: Implemented 4 slash commands: /ground, /sweep, /audit-agents, /implement. All verified accessible via Claude Code, Pi, and Codex symlinks.

title: Centralize AGENTS.md guidance across repos
description: Moved common guidance (beads, cass, agent-mail, bv, ast-grep, ubs, worktree) to central agent-config. Removed duplicates from project AGENTS.md files.
close_reason: Completed: Centralized guidance in agent-config, cleaned orchestrator, mcp_agent_mail, cass, groovetech-aivu-hls-tools, opencode-config

title: Select and shape a low-maintenance cross-machine config control plane
description: Research and shape whether to continue current agent-config approach, evolve it with stronger primitives, or adopt a better-fit system so setup/parity becomes rock-solid with minimal ongoing developer attention.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Bead-linked spec/plan/tasks exist with phased migration options and decision gates. 2) Recommendation matrix compares at least current model, hybrid upgrade path, and full replacement path. 3) Plan protects existing working setup while reducing maintenance overhead. 4) Success metrics and rollback strategy are explicit.
notes:
Created planning artifacts: specs/002-low-maintenance-config-control-plane/{spec.md,plan.md,tasks.md,research.md}. Recommendation path: hybrid (agent-config + chezmoi + brew bundle + mise) with decision gates before escalation to Nix.
Added explicit North Star hard-gate criteria to spec/plan/tasks: anti-bloat constraints, complexity budget checks, and phase stop/revert conditions tied to measurable maintenance-time outcomes.
close_reason: Decision Gate A passes: current stack + bootstrap.sh meets all success metrics (setup <5min, parity check <30sec, maintenance <1hr/mo). Phases 2-4 (chezmoi/brew/mise/nix) not warranted — would violate North Star filter. All exit criteria met.

title: Evaluate future hook-equivalent implementation for Codex vs Pi
description: Documented that Pi has hook-like interception through extensions and event handlers (pi.on for tool_call/tool_result/session events), while Codex CLI/docs expose no built-in hook/event/matcher framework. User wants to test Codex first before implementing any cross-agent hook tooling.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Verify Pi extension event hooks can replicate PostToolUse-like behavior for bash/tool-result flows. 2) Verify Codex has no native hook registry in CLI/config and only supports wrapper/prompt policies. 3) Provide an implementation plan only after Claude Code testing confirms feasibility.
notes: Evidence from local docs: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/*.ts, ~/.codex/config.toml; CLI references: pi --help, codex --help, codex features --help
close_reason: Research complete. Pi has full event hooks (tool_call/tool_result with blocking). Codex has zero hook support. No cross-agent hook tooling warranted until Codex adds an event system. Pi and Claude Code hooks remain agent-specific.

title: Fix Codex instructions path
description: Codex uses ~/.codex/AGENTS.md, not ~/.codex/instructions.md. Updated install.sh and README.md to use the correct path.

title: Unify skills across all AI coding agents
description:
Centralize skills in ~/.agent-config/skills/ with symlinks so all agents (Claude Code, Codex, Pi) share the same skills.

## Completed
- Created ~/.agent-config/skills/ with categories: cc3/, tools/, swift/, personal/, ralph-o/
- Added 166 skills total
- Added 27 ralph-o presets
- Created top-level symlinks for flat structure (Claude Code compatibility)
- Configured Pi to only scan ~/.pi/agent/skills/ (avoid duplicates)
- Fixed Pi-agent compatibility issues
- Removed duplicate checkpoint/finalize skills (kept as commands)
